summary: Outstanding sound‑board capture of a spectacular second set – monster Not Fade Away, gorgeous Attics of My Life and a flawless NFA→St Stephen segue.
review: The recording is praised as an excellent SBD master with crystal‑clear mix and outrageous guitar tone.  Musically the show shines on the blazing Not Fade Away jam, the beautiful rendition of Attics of My Life, and tight interplay between Jerry and Bob that carries through a seamless transition into St Stephen, making this set one of the standouts of 1970.

ai_rating.rationale: All eight reviews are 4‑5 stars and uniformly laud both performance and audio quality, giving a strong consensus for a high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the mix as great, tone captured outrageously well; sourced from original Ampex 456 tape at 7½ ips and transferred cleanly with no noted flaws.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second‑set energy is highlighted by a soaring Not Fade Away jam that segues flawlessly into St Stephen, with strong interludes on Cumberland and a beautiful Attics of My Life; overall the set is described as fierce, confident, and one of the year’s best.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar tone is repeatedly praised, especially during the Not Fade Away jam and the seamless NFA→St Stephen transition.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir is noted as "on top of his game" with a standout guitar performance on Not Fade Away and tight interplay with Jerry.
